metadata.summary: Five privacy principles (transparency, control, minimization, security, deletion) and split-agent architecture for sensitive VUI operations.

content.title: VUI Privacy and Security
content.summary: Five privacy principles (transparency, control, minimization, security, deletion) and a split-agent architecture for sensitive VUI operations: a policy classifier decides "is this turn sensitive?" and an executor crafts the response — the executor never sees raw sensitive values. Redaction must happen pre-storage and pre-LLM-context, not post-hoc. Two-channel confirmation (voice initiates, SMS/push confirms) prevents voice-spoofing attacks on destructive operations.

why.p: Voice data is ambient and persistent. ASR transcripts may contain PII, health, or financial data that flows silently into LLM context, observability logs, and training pipelines. A single-agent design that both classifies sensitivity and generates responses creates a path for PII leakage in rationale fields. Splitting into classifier + executor with structured outputs (no free-text echo of user data) blocks the leak at the architecture level.

list.item: Designing voice features that handle PII, health, financial, or auth-sensitive data.
Drafting privacy disclosures, consent flows, and data-retention copy for voice products.
Auditing existing VUI for missing trust indicators (listening indicator, stop command, history access).
Building agentic pipelines where ASR transcripts feed LLM calls and require redaction first.
Pure text chatbots — covered by general data-privacy methodologies, not VUI specifics.
One-off internal tools with no user voice capture (agent-only TTS announcements).
Formal compliance scoping (GDPR DPIA, HIPAA controls) — VUI principles inform but do not replace formal assessment.

rule.statement: Disclose what voice data is collected, how it is used, and for how long — before the user first speaks, not buried in terms.
rule.rationale: Transparency is both a regulatory requirement (GDPR Art. 13) and a user trust baseline; late disclosure causes opt-out and product abandonment.

rule.statement: Collect only the audio and transcript data necessary for the immediate task; do not retain raw audio for analytics by default.
rule.rationale: Data minimization reduces regulatory exposure and breach impact; users are more willing to use voice features when they know data is not persisted indefinitely.

rule.statement: Encrypt all voice data at rest and in transit; use envelope encryption with per-device keys for recordings.
rule.rationale: Voice recordings are biometric-adjacent data; a breach without encryption exposes users to identification and voice-cloning attacks.

rule.statement: Provide a user-accessible voice history view backed by a separate auditable store; agents must fetch it, never reconstruct or summarize from LLM context.
rule.rationale: If the agent can summarize history from context, it can hallucinate it; a separate store ensures accuracy and supports GDPR/CCPA right-of-access requests.

rule.statement: Implement a hard-delete endpoint for voice history that removes raw audio, transcripts, and derived embeddings; confirm deletion to the user.
rule.rationale: GDPR Art. 17 right to erasure applies to voice data; embeddings derived from voice are biometric data with a separate retention clock.

rule.statement: Show a visual indicator (LED ring or on-screen icon) whenever the microphone is active; provide an audio confirmation when it stops.
rule.rationale: Users cannot see whether a microphone is active; a visual/audio indicator is the primary signal that gives users confidence they are not being recorded covertly.

rule.statement: Make the "stop listening" command work without a wake word and without TTS acknowledgement; provide a silent, immediate kill switch.
rule.rationale: A stop command that requires hearing the wake word first defeats its purpose; users in sensitive situations need an immediate, unconditional off switch.

rule.statement: For banking, health, and personal data operations: require step-up authentication, mask spoken sensitive values, and confirm before executing.
rule.rationale: Voice is inherently ambient; a second channel (push notification or SMS) that must be acknowledged prevents voice-spoofing attacks from completing high-stakes actions.

rule.statement: Treat voice biometric IDs as biometric data under GDPR/CCPA — separate consent, separate retention clock, separate deletion API.
rule.rationale: Biometric data has heightened regulatory protection; co-mingling it with regular user data risks using the wrong retention period and deletion scope.

rule.statement: Split every sensitive VUI flow into a policy-classifier agent and an executor agent; the executor never receives raw sensitive slot values.
rule.rationale: A single agent that both classifies sensitivity and generates responses creates a path for PII to appear in "thought", "rationale", or log fields; the split eliminates that path structurally.

rule.statement: Redact sensitive slots pre-storage and pre-LLM-context, not post-hoc.
rule.rationale: Post-hoc redaction fails if the LLM already cached the value or included it in a prior streaming chunk; pre-redaction is the only reliable boundary.

antipattern.description: Classifier returns the original utterance text in any field.
antipattern.reason: Any echo of user text in structured output creates a PII logging path; force structured outputs that reference slot names, not values.

rule.statement: For destructive or irreversible voice operations, require confirmation via a second channel (SMS or push notification) — not a follow-up voice prompt.
rule.rationale: Voice spoofing can complete a two-turn voice-only confirmation; a second channel requires device-level possession that a spoofed voice cannot satisfy.

rule.statement: After step-up authentication succeeds, invalidate the cached LLM context; do not allow the "trusted" flag to persist across unrelated turns.
rule.rationale: A persistent auth flag in context enables session hijacking — any subsequent turn in the same session would bypass authentication.

antipattern.description: LLM leaks sensitive slot values into "rationale" or "thought" fields of structured output.
antipattern.reason: Forbid free-text reasoning that quotes user data; force structured outputs that reference slot names only, validated by schema before logging.

antipattern.description: ASR transcripts contain partial sensitive data ("my card is four nine one two...") that regex misses.
antipattern.reason: Combine word-level NER with intent-aware slot deletion; regex alone cannot handle spoken partial values or transposed digits.

antipattern.description: Observability tools (Datadog, Sentry) receive prompt+response containing sensitive data.
antipattern.reason: Configure log scrubbers at the SDK layer before the log sink; agents cannot reliably self-redact in observability calls made by the framework.

antipattern.description: Caching layer re-emits PII because the cache key is the redacted text but the cached value contains the original.
antipattern.reason: Always cache on redacted outputs only; never use unredacted inputs as cache keys with redacted-output values.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: Split privacy/security into a policy agent that decides "is this turn sensitive?" plus an executor agent that crafts the response respecting the policy. The policy agent reads intent + slot values + user profile flags and emits {sensitive:bool, requires_auth:bool, mask_fields:[...], log_level:"redact"|"full"}. The executor consumes that output and never sees raw sensitive values for logging paths. Redaction must happen pre-storage and pre-LLM-context, not post-hoc.

list.item: vui-privacy-classifier — haiku; binary/structured classification of utterances against a policy schema.
vui-redactor — haiku; deterministic regex+LLM hybrid that masks PII in transcripts before downstream use.
consent-copywriter — sonnet; drafts disclosure scripts tuned for plain-language readability.
auth-flow-designer — sonnet; produces step-up auth dialogs (PIN, biometric, fallback) with branch coverage.

section.code: You are vui-privacy-classifier. Given the user utterance and current slot map, return STRICT JSON: {sensitive: bool, categories: [pii|health|financial|auth|other], requires_step_up_auth: bool, mask_in_logs: [slot_names], rationale: <=20 words}. Never echo the utterance text in fields other than `rationale`.

section.name: cli-tools-and-services
section.p: CLI tools for PII detection and redaction:

list.item: presidio-cli (Microsoft Presidio) — PII detection/redaction in transcripts. Install: pip install presidio-analyzer presidio-anonymizer. Docs: microsoft.github.io/presidio
aws cli (Comprehend, Transcribe redaction) — Built-in PII/PHI redaction in ASR. Docs: docs.aws.amazon.com/transcribe/latest/dg/pii-redaction.html
gcloud dlp — Google DLP API for transcript scanning. Docs: cloud.google.com/dlp/docs/concepts-info-types
op (1Password CLI) — Pull voice-bot secrets at runtime, never in repo. Docs: developer.1password.com/docs/cli/
audit-cli (custom) — Diff voice-history exports between releases for retention regressions. Internal use only.
step (smallstep) — Generate per-device certs for voice clients. Docs: smallstep.com/docs/step-cli
Microsoft Presidio (OSS) — Python SDK + REST. Custom recognizers for domain PII (loyalty IDs, MRNs).
AWS Transcribe (SaaS) — REST + boto3. Real-time and batch redaction modes.
Google DLP (SaaS) — REST. 100+ infoTypes; supports custom patterns.
OneTrust / Transcend (SaaS) — REST for DSAR ops. Use for "delete voice history" request automation.
Pindrop / Nuance Gatekeeper (SaaS) — Voice biometrics + spoof detection.
Vault by HashiCorp (OSS) — REST/CLI. Encrypt voice recordings at rest with envelope keys.
Default to ephemeral memory for sensitive turns: keep them in the dialog manager only, never persist to logs or vector stores.
Two-channel confirmation for destructive/irreversible operations: voice initiates, push notification or SMS confirms — voice spoofing alone cannot complete the action.
Make the "stop listening" command work without wake word and without TTS reply (silent acknowledgement) so users feel they have a hard kill switch.
Maintain a per-user "voice history" endpoint that reads from a separate auditable store; agents must never invent or summarize the history — only fetch.
Run a quarterly red-team: prompt the agent to read back the last few turns or "remind me what I told you about my card." If it succeeds, the redaction layer is broken.
Treat voice biometric IDs as biometric data under GDPR/CCPA — separate consent, separate retention clock, separate deletion API.

section.name: agent-limitations-and-gotchas
section.p: Agent integration notes from practical deployment:
Principles are jurisdiction-agnostic; GDPR Art.9, COPPA, HIPAA, EU AI Act voice biometrics rules require their own checklists.
"Mask spoken sensitive data" is hard at TTS layer — most engines do not support inline redaction tokens; needs upstream substitution.
Wake-word indicator design assumes hardware control (LED, ring); web/embedded VUI with no hardware affordance must use audio-only indicators.
Voice biometric authentication is treated as one option; principles do not cover spoof/replay defense or liveness checks.
LLMs leak sensitive slots into "rationale" or "thought" fields. Forbid free-text reasoning that quotes user data; force structured outputs without echo.
A model asked "is this sensitive?" without examples drifts — pin a few-shot block with positive and negative cases per category.
Caching layers (LLM response cache, prompt cache) can re-emit redacted PII if the cache key is the redacted text but the value contains the original. Always cache on redacted outputs only.
ASR transcripts may include partial redactions ("my card is four nine one two..."); regex misses these. Combine word-level NER with intent-aware slot deletion.
Step-up auth must invalidate the cached LLM context after success — otherwise an attacker who passes auth once retains a "trusted" flag for the whole session.
Voice history exports for DSAR must be human-readable transcripts, not opaque audio blobs. Pre-generate text alongside audio at ingest.
Logs to observability tools (Datadog, Sentry) regularly contain prompt+response. Configure log scrubbers at the SDK layer; do not rely on agents to self-redact in logs.

template.purpose: Regex + slot-map redactor that strips PII from transcripts before LLM or log ingestion.
Structured-output prompt for a vui-privacy-classifier subagent.
